message:
feat(cli): pre-TUI startup banner, smarter reservation validation, and port migration to 8899

Pre-TUI startup banner (cli/lib.rs): Emit the redesigned ASCII-art startup
banner *before* entering raw-mode TUI, so operators see server config and
database stats in their scrollback even if the TUI later clears the screen.
The banner queries lightweight COUNT(*) stats from the database and renders
via the same render_startup_banner() used by the dashboard, ensuring visual
consistency. Only emitted when stdout is a TTY and TUI mode is enabled.
Cargo.lock gains a tokio dependency for the CLI crate to support the async
database query.

Reservation validation (tools/reservations.rs): Feed patterns through
CompiledPattern::new() before running suspicious-pattern checks. This means
"src/../../../etc/passwd" is first normalized to "etc/passwd", allowing the
too-broad and short-wildcard heuristics to operate on the canonical form.
Error messages now show both the original and normalized pattern for
debuggability. Absolute-path detection still runs against the raw input
since normalization strips leading slashes. Also replace .max().min() TTL
clamping with the more idiomatic .clamp() method.

Resource formatting (tools/resources.rs): Break long ternary limit-parsing
closures across multiple lines for readability. No logic change — the
zero-means-unlimited fallback and default of 20 are preserved across inbox,
mailbox, mailbox_with_commits, outbox, urgent_unread, ack_required,
acks_stale, and ack_overdue endpoints.

MCP config port migration (codex/cursor/gemini.mcp.json): Update the
default server endpoint from port 8765 to 8899. This aligns the checked-in
configs with the new port assignment established during the recent install
system overhaul, avoiding connection failures for fresh checkouts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
